template_block.name: global-documentation-ruleset
template_block.title: Global Documentation Ruleset
template_block.statement: The rules in global-documentation.xml are mandatory for all artefact creation, across:

checklist.entry: ALL modes (TEAM MODE and all discipline modes),
ALL service phases (Pre‑Discovery, Discovery, Alpha, Beta, Live),

ALL types of artefacts including PowerPoint decks, structured documents, reports, research outputs, BA artefacts, PM artefacts, SD artefacts, accessibility audits, and any multi‑section or multi‑slide deliverable.

template_block.statement: Nothing in any other rule file may disable, weaken, bypass, or contradict the requirements in this file.
Whenever an output constitutes an artefact, the assistant must:

checklist.entry: Automatically apply the structure, formatting, accessibility, and traceability rules defined here without waiting for user instruction.
Resolve any conflict between discipline‑specific structure and this file using:

template_block.statement: "Most protective documentation rule wins".

checklist.entry: Regenerate (or decline) any requested artefact that would otherwise violate these rules.
Treat these rules as binding in all modes, including default TEAM MODE.

template_block.statement: Linting rules in agent-lint.config.xml enforce compliance and must be treated as non‑optional.

template_block.name: powerpoint-and-information-deck-standards
template_block.title: PowerPoint & Information Deck Standards
template_block.statement: Whenever the Product Assistant Agent creates a PowerPoint document, it must use the mandated DaaS presentation template and strictly follow all rules in this section.

Source template: DaaS‑presentation.pptx* [1](https://ukhomeoffice.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/prog1896/SiteAssets/Document_Templates/DaaS-presentation.pptx?d=w931cec9d07754129b1f748b2027bede6&csf=1&web=1&e=oS9USS)
Master: “Slide template for presentations — [Presenter] Home Office Digital”

purpose.statement: These rules standardise the creation of PowerPoint decks by ensuring:

checklist.entry: visual consistency
accessibility compliance
clear communication
correct use of typefaces, colours, and layouts

purpose.statement: All slide authors and automated agents must follow this ruleset.

checklist.entry: The agent must always begin by loading DaaS-presentation.pptx.
This file’s Slide Masters define the only permissible layouts.

template_block.name: slide-replacement-rule
template_block.title: Slide Replacement Rule
template_block.statement: When generating a PowerPoint file:

checklist.entry: The agent must create a copy of DaaS-presentation.pptx.
The agent must delete all existing slides from the copy.
The agent must then create only new slides based on user‑requested content.
The agent must use master layouts, not ad‑hoc shapes or manual styling.

template_block.statement: Appending new slides to the end of the template is not permitted.

template_block.name: master-aware-layout-selection-python-reference
template_block.title: Master‑aware layout selection (Python reference)
template_block.statement: The Product Assistant must always select PowerPoint layouts by master + layout name, never by index.
For the DaaS-presentation.pptx template, the slide masters and their layouts are:

```text Master 0 (light theme) 0 Title and Content 1 1_White Section Header 2 Two Content 3 Title Only 4 Large photo with text 5 Quote with text split screen 6 Image and text split screen 7 Image with caption 8 Blank + Footer 9 Blank
Master 1 (dark theme) 0 Title Slide 1 Section Header 2 Big statistic 3 Blank + Footer 4 Blank ```

template_block.name: master-and-layout-discovery-python
template_block.title: Master and layout discovery (Python)
template_block.statement: The agent MUST use logic equivalent to this when creating decks:
```python from pptx import Presentation
TEMPLATE_PATH = "DaaS-presentation.pptx" prs = Presentation(TEMPLATE_PATH)

template_block.name: remove-all-existing-slides-from-the-copied-template
template_block.title: Remove all existing slides from the copied template
template_block.statement: for i in range(len(prs.slides) - 1, -1, -1): r_id = prs.slides._sldIdLst[i].rId prs.part.drop_rel(r_id) del prs.slides._sldIdLst[i]
def _norm(name: str) -> str: """Normalise a layout name for comparison.""" return name.strip().lower()

def find_master_with_layout(prs: Presentation, layout_name: str): """Return the slide master that owns a layout with the given name.""" target = _norm(layout_name) for master in prs.slide_masters: for lo in master.slide_layouts: if _norm(lo.name) == target: return master raise ValueError(f"No master contains a layout named '{layout_name}'")

def find_layout_in_master(master, layout_name: str): """Return a specific layout from the given master by name.""" target = _norm(layout_name) for lo in master.slide_layouts: if _norm(lo.name) == target: return lo raise KeyError(f"Master {master} has no layout named '{layout_name}'")

template_block.name: identify-dark-and-light-masters-by-canonical-layouts
template_block.title: ) Identify dark and light masters by canonical layouts
template_block.statement: dark_master = find_master_with_layout(prs, "Title Slide") light_master = find_master_with_layout(prs, "Title and Content")

template_block.name: resolve-named-layouts-from-the-appropriate-master
template_block.title: ) Resolve named layouts from the appropriate master
template_block.statement: layouts = { # Dark master (DaaS_Dark_Title) "title-slide" : find_layout_in_master(dark_master, "Title Slide"), "dark-section-header" : find_layout_in_master(dark_master, "Section Header"), "dark-big-stat" : find_layout_in_master(dark_master, "Big statistic"), "dark-blank-footer" : find_layout_in_master(dark_master, "Blank + Footer"), "dark-blank" : find_layout_in_master(dark_master, "Blank"),

# Light master (DaaS_Light) "content" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Title and Content"), "light-section-header": find_layout_in_master(light_master, "1_White Section Header"), "two-content" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Two Content"), "light-title-only" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Title Only"), "photo-with-text" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Large photo with text "), "quote-split" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Quote with text split screen"), "image-text-split" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Image and text split screen"), "image-caption" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Image with caption"), "light-blank-footer" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Blank + Footer"), "light-blank" : find_layout_in_master(light_master, "Blank"), } ```
Lint rule: the agent MUST NOT silently fall back to prs.slide_layouts[0]. If any required layout is missing, generation should be treated as a hard error and flagged for human review.

examples.example_context: All helper functions used by the agent MUST reference the layouts map above, never numeric indexes, e.g.:

```python def add_title_slide(prs: Presentation, title: str, subtitle: str | None = None): slide = prs.slides.add_slide(layouts["title-slide"]) slide.shapes.title.text = title if subtitle: for ph in slide.placeholders: if ph.placeholder_format.idx != 0: ph.text = subtitle break

def add_title_and_content(prs: Presentation, title: str, lines: list[str]): slide = prs.slides.add_slide(layouts["content"]) slide.shapes.title.text = title body = next(ph for ph in slide.placeholders if ph.placeholder_format.idx != 0) tf = body.text_frame tf.clear() first = True for line in lines: if first: tf.text = line first = False else: p = tf.add_paragraph() p.text = line p.level = 0
def add_two_column(prs: Presentation, title: str, left_lines: list[str], right_lines: list[str]): slide = prs.slides.add_slide(layouts["two-content"]) slide.shapes.title.text = title
placeholders = [ph for ph in slide.placeholders if ph.placeholder_format.idx != 0] left_ph, right_ph = placeholders[0], placeholders[1]

for ph, lines in ((left_ph, left_lines), (right_ph, right_lines)): tf = ph.text_frame tf.clear() first = True for line in lines: if first: tf.text = line first = False else: p = tf.add_paragraph() p.text = line p.level = 0 ```
The above is reference logic; implementations may vary but MUST preserve:

example_list.example: No fallback to arbitrary layouts without raising an error

template_block.name: layout-usage-rules
template_block.title: Layout usage rules
template_block.statement: The following rules define when the Product Assistant must use each layout from DaaS-presentation.pptx.

template_block.name: title-slide-layouts-title-slide
template_block.title: **Title Slide — `layouts["title-slide"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: The first slide of any deck
Primary title decks (e.g. “Third Country National Data Discovery – Programme Board briefing”)

template_block.statement: Do not use for minor section headings.

template_block.name: section-header-layouts-dark-section-header
template_block.title: **Section Header — `layouts["dark-section-header"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: Major section breaks in a deck
e.g. “As‑Is Landscape”, “User Research Insights”, “Solution Options”
Slides containing only a short heading and optional one‑line sub‑text

template_block.name: big-statistic-layouts-dark-big-stat
template_block.title: **Big statistic — `layouts["dark-big-stat"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: Single hero metrics (e.g. “900K TCN records”, “60K ACRO requests per year”)
At most one short supporting line

template_block.name: blank-footer-blank-layouts-dark-blank-footer-layouts-dark-blank
template_block.title: **Blank + Footer / Blank — `layouts["dark-blank-footer"]`, `layouts["dark-blank"]`**
template_block.statement: Use only when:

checklist.entry: A fully custom visual or diagram is needed on a dark background
No standard layout can support the design

template_block.statement: Avoid normal text content on these.

template_block.name: title-and-content-layouts-content
template_block.title: **Title and Content — `layouts["content"]`**
template_block.statement: Default for most slides. Use for:

checklist.entry: Standard “Title + body” slides:
Executive summaries
Problem statements
Evidence summaries
User insights
Decisions and next steps
Bullet lists that fit cleanly in a single column

template_block.statement: If content is dense or naturally split into two themes, prefer Two Content.

template_block.name: 1-white-section-header-layouts-light-section-header
template_block.title: **1_White Section Header — `layouts["light-section-header"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: Section breaks on a light theme
e.g. “Appendix”, “Detailed Metrics”, “Personas”

template_block.statement: Do not mix dark and light section headers at the same hierarchy level within the same deck.

template_block.name: two-content-layouts-two-content
template_block.title: **Two Content — `layouts["two-content"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: Parallel or paired content, such as:
“Risks / Mitigations”
“Current state / Future state”
“User needs / Pain points”
“Pros / Cons”
When a single column would become too long to scan easily

template_block.statement: Each column should contain short, single‑sentence bullets.

template_block.name: title-only-layouts-light-title-only
template_block.title: **Title Only — `layouts["light-title-only"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: Light‑theme divider slides requiring only a title
Short call‑outs where emphasis is on the heading
End slides such as “Thank you / Questions” on a light background

template_block.statement: Do not use this as a replacement for the main dark Title Slide.

template_block.name: large-photo-with-text-layouts-photo-with-text
template_block.title: **Large photo with text — `layouts["photo-with-text"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: Slides where a single image is central and supported by a small block of text
e.g. a user environment photo, a key system, or conceptual illustration
3–5 bullets or 1–2 short paragraphs only

template_block.statement: The image must be meaningful, not purely decorative.

template_block.name: quote-with-text-split-screen-layouts-quote-split
template_block.title: **Quote with text split screen — `layouts["quote-split"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: A single, prominent user or stakeholder quote on one side with interpretation on the other
Highlighting a representative insight that needs explanation

template_block.statement: Keep quotes short and focused; avoid stacking multiple quotes.

template_block.name: image-and-text-split-screen-layouts-image-text-split
template_block.title: **Image and text split screen — `layouts["image-text-split"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: Slides where image and text are equally important
e.g. a process diagram with bullet commentary
3–6 bullets or one concise paragraph

template_block.name: image-with-caption-layouts-image-caption
template_block.title: **Image with caption — `layouts["image-caption"]`**
template_block.statement: Use for:

checklist.entry: A single image where the caption carries the explanation
e.g. screenshots, persona tiles, simple diagrams

template_block.statement: Caption text must be placed in the branded caption box and follow caption style.

template_block.name: blank-footer-blank-layouts-light-blank-footer-layouts-light-blank
template_block.title: **Blank + Footer / Blank — `layouts["light-blank-footer"]`, `layouts["light-blank"]`**
template_block.statement: Use only when:

checklist.entry: Creating a custom diagram or layout that cannot reuse any standard layout
The footer is still required (choose Blank + Footer)

template_block.statement: Avoid using these as a shortcut for standard text slides.

template_block.name: layout-selection-rules-summary
template_block.title: Layout selection rules (summary)
template_block.statement: When the agent chooses layouts, it MUST follow:

checklist.entry: First slide → Title Slide from dark master (layouts["title-slide"]).
Most content slides → Title and Content from light master (layouts["content"]).
Parallel content → Two Content from light master (layouts["two-content"]).
Dark theme → Section Header from dark master (layouts["dark-section-header"]).
Light theme → 1_White Section Header from light master (layouts["light-section-header"]).
Big statistic (layouts["dark-big-stat"])

template_block.name: image-or-quote-driven-slides
template_block.title: Image‑ or quote‑driven slides
template_block.statement: Use one of:

checklist.entry: Large photo with text
Quote with text split screen
Image and text split screen
Image with caption

template_block.name: custom-visuals-only
template_block.title: Custom visuals only

checklist.entry: Blank or Blank + Footer layouts
Use only when no other layout fits.

template_block.statement: Any layout choice outside these rules is a design exception and must be validated manually.

checklist.entry: Titles: UD Digi Kyohasho NP‑B
Body text: Helvetica
Title‑only slides: 48pt minimum
Body text: 16pt minimum
Titles should be as large as possible while preserving layout integrity.
Aim for approximately seven words per line for large headings.

template_block.name: alignment
template_block.title: Alignment
template_block.statement: All body text must be:

checklist.entry: Vertically top‑aligned
Left‑aligned

template_block.name: bullet-points
template_block.title: Bullet Points
template_block.statement: Bullet text must:

checklist.entry: Run on from the lead‑in line
Contain no semicolons
Be limited to one sentence
Be concise and scannable

template_block.name: highlight-slides
template_block.title: Highlight Slides
template_block.statement: For single high‑impact data points, use the dedicated large‑number style (e.g., “90%”).

template_block.name: accessibility-checker
template_block.title: Accessibility Checker
template_block.statement: All decks must pass the built‑in checker: Review → Check Accessibility

checklist.entry: Use white text on darker brand colours
Use black text on lighter brand colours
Only colours from the official brand palette may be used.
No custom colours or shading.
Backgrounds must remain consistent with the master layout defaults.
Images should fill the entire slide edge‑to‑edge when appropriate.
When full‑bleed is not suitable, use the specific inset‑image layout from the template.
Captions must appear inside a brand‑coloured caption box.
Caption text must be white.
Only icons included in the DaaS template may be used.
No third‑party iconography.
Only the illustrated persona assets provided in the official template may be used.
No stock photography or alternative avatar styles.

template_block.name: tables
template_block.title: Tables
template_block.statement: Tables must follow the template’s approved table style:

checklist.entry: Header row
Consistent spacing
Clear grid
Use only the custom bar‑indicator graphics provided in the template.
For layered concepts, use the official concentric‑ring diagram graphic.

template_block.name: mixed-media-layouts
template_block.title: Mixed Media Layouts
template_block.statement: When combining images and text:

checklist.entry: Use the predefined mixed‑media layout with bullets on one side and an image pane on the other.

template_block.name: prohibited-practices
template_block.title: Prohibited Practices
template_block.statement: The following are not permitted:

checklist.entry: Any fonts not supplied by the DaaS template
Body text smaller than 16pt
Unapproved colour palettes
Low‑contrast text or backgrounds
Bullets containing semicolons
Bullets containing more than one sentence
Use of icons or personas outside the approved library
Manually formatted shapes replacing proper master‑layout placeholders
Adding slides that do not originate from a DaaS slide master layout

template_block.name: enforcement
template_block.title: Enforcement
template_block.statement: These rules are mandatory for the Product Assistant Agent. Any PowerPoint output must satisfy:

checklist.entry: Correct template use
Correct slide‑master layout mapping
Full replacement of existing template slides
Typography, colour, and accessibility compliance
AND the universal applicability rules at the beginning of this global-documentation.xml ruleset (mandatory for all artefacts)

template_block.statement: Failure to satisfy these rules must result in regeneration of the deck.
